message:
feat(fcp-sdk): add ConnectorApp trait and execution-form-neutral contract module, rebrand to FCP3

Introduce the ConnectorApp trait — the execution-form-neutral authoring
contract for FCP3 connectors. Where FcpConnector is the low-level
runtime surface, ConnectorApp sits above it to let authors declare
execution forms (Native, WASI, etc.), archetypes, state models, and
semantic surfaces.

fcp-sdk/src/contract.rs (new, 776 lines):
- ConnectorApp trait with describe(), contract_introspection(), and
  capability_catalog_for() methods
- ConnectorAppContract: bundles descriptor + introspection + capability
  catalog into a single publishable artifact
- ConnectorAppDescriptor: execution form, archetype, state model
- 11 "surface" types: InvokeSurface, StreamingSurface, DrainSurface,
  CheckpointSurface, ResumeSurface, ProvisioningSurface, BudgetSurface,
  EvidenceSurface, DiagnosticsSurface, ConnectorOperationCapability,
  ConnectorCapabilityCatalog
- Unit tests for contract construction and capability deduplication

fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs:
- Rebrand doc comments from FCP2 to FCP3
- Restructure re-exports: execution/lifecycle types from fcp-kernel
  (primary), policy/provenance from fcp-core (secondary)
- Add pub mod contract and re-export all contract types
- New re-exports: CapabilityId, RiskLevel, SafetyTier, HumanPrompt,
  ProvisioningRecipe, Lease types, UsageBudget types, Checkpoint types

fcp-sdk/src/prelude.rs:
- Add ConnectorApp to core traits
- Add provisioning, cost/availability, contract, checkpoint/lease/budget
  primitives to prelude

fcp-sdk/tests/sdk_trait_tests.rs:
- Implement ConnectorApp for MinimalConnector
- test_connector_app_contract and capability dedup tests

Cargo.toml: Rebrand description, add fcp-kernel dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
-//! FCP2 Connector SDK
+//! FCP3 Connector SDK
 //!
-//! This crate provides the SDK for authoring FCP2-compliant connectors.
-//! It re-exports key types from `fcp-core` and provides convenience utilities
-//! to make implementing connectors easy and correct.
+//! This crate provides the SDK for authoring FCP3-compliant connector
+//! applications. It re-exports platform-owned execution types from
+//! `fcp-kernel`, keeps policy and evidence types available from the lower
+//! layers, and exposes authoring utilities that make the shared runtime model
+//! explicit instead of host-specific.
 //!
 //! # Quick Start
 //!
@@ -28,17 +30,36 @@
 //!
 //!     // ... implement other methods
 //! }
+//!
+//! impl ConnectorApp for MyConnector {
+//!     fn describe(&self) -> ConnectorAppDescriptor {
+//!         ConnectorAppDescriptor::new(self.id().clone())
+//!             .with_execution_form(ConnectorRuntimeFormat::Native)
+//!             .with_archetype(ConnectorArchetype::Operational)
+//!             .with_state_model(ConnectorStateModel::Stateless)
+//!             .with_diagnostics(
+//!                 DiagnosticsSurface::connector_author_defaults()
+//!                     .with_fixture_scenario("my_connector.happy_path"),
+//!             )
+//!     }
+//! }
 //! ```
 //!
 //! # Architecture
 //!
 //! The SDK is structured around:
 //!
-//! - **[`FcpConnector`]**: The core trait all connectors must implement
+//! - **[`FcpConnector`]**: The low-level runtime trait all connectors implement
+//! - **[`ConnectorApp`]**: The execution-form-neutral connector app contract
+//! - **[`ConnectorAppContract`]**: The self-contained published contract carrying
+//!   both runtime semantics and full connector introspection
 //! - **[`BaseConnector`]**: A base implementation with common functionality
 //! - **[`FcpError`]**: Structured error types with recovery hints
 //! - **Archetype traits**: [`Streaming`], [`Bidirectional`], [`Polling`], [`Webhook`]
 //!
+//! Connector authors should treat [`ConnectorApp`] as the stable authoring
+//! contract and [`FcpConnector`] as the low-level runtime surface beneath it.
+//!
 //! # Error Handling
 //!
 //! All errors use the FCP error taxonomy:
@@ -58,98 +79,142 @@
